title: Add unit/integration tests for empty-log initialization and transaction validation failures
summary: Add focused tests covering startup from an initialized-but-empty tx_event_log.jsonl and validation failures for transaction-related operations so the 0.4.5 fixes are protected by regression coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover startup and/or rebuild behavior when tx_event_log.jsonl exists and is empty.
Tests assert that an empty tx_event_log.jsonl is treated as a valid initialized baseline rather than a fatal error.
Tests distinguish missing, empty, and malformed tx_event_log.jsonl cases where the implementation exposes different behavior.
Tests cover required validation for transaction-related inputs including actor, payload, non-empty session_id, required state, and supported timezone values.
Validation failure assertions use explicit error messages aligned with the documented transaction workflow.
Added tests contribute toward maintaining total project coverage at or above 90%.

steps.description: Add tests for initialized empty-log handling in startup, load, and/or rebuild paths.
Add tests that compare empty-log behavior against missing-log and malformed-log behavior where applicable.
Add validation tests for transaction-related inputs such as actor, payload, session_id, state, and timezone.
Assert clear failure messages and review coverage impact for the new validation tests.

notes: Keep tests focused on validation and initialization behavior; ordering/state-transition regressions are handled by later tickets.
Prefer small unit tests with explicit assertions over broad end-to-end fixtures unless integration coverage is required for empty-log startup behavior.
Use the updated draft error inventory as the source for regression scenarios.
